text: Highlight Clip Arthur Hayes: Trump Family Turned to Crypto After Being Debanked BitMEX founder Arthur Hayes explains that Donald Trump and his campaign shifted to pro-crypto rhetoric in 2024 because his family was antagonized and debanked by the traditional banking system. Facing lawsuits and frozen assets, they realized the true value of assets existing outside state control. Hayes believes without being outside the regulatory apparatus, Bitcoin is just another regular asset. He also noted Bitcoin's price doesn't care about regulations, only about money printing. — link

text: It Always Comes Back to Network Effects > Systems of record are no longer defensible. Agents can now raid the systems of record by manually extracting all of the data, row by row, and putting it elsewhere. > To make a system of record actually defensible in an agent-first world, you have to have network effects. > Agent can copy every row in your CRM, but can't make your customers open the new vendor's emails. > They can scrape Instagram's entire graph but can't make friends open a different app > Can distill models but can't replicate the user base generating preference and interaction data that compounds the next version. Source:

text: The Next Biggest Moat in AI The opportunity now is not to become the next OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Tesla. But to ask what kind of company has not been possible before, and what kind of person has been waiting for it to exist. > The piece argues that as AI capabilities, interfaces, and products converge and become easily copyable, the enduring competitive moat is the shape of the company itself. > How the company attracts top talent, distributes authority, and compounds judgment over time. > The old talent market rewarded companies that made people feel chosen. > The next one will reward companies built in shapes the old market could not have produced, and the people inside them will become something the old shapes could not have made possible Source:

text: Four Culture Fixes > Robin Hanson argues humanity has broken its cultural evolution, its ability to evolve and sustain key values, due to declining variety, weakened selection pressures, rapid environmental change, and excessive internal drift. > "Unless we achieve human level AI soon, our dominant world civ's population seem likely to decline, to be replaced by fertile insular religious subcultures like the Amish and Haredim". He proposes four potential fixes: • Creating durable high-fertility subcultures • Extending capitalism into more domains • Adaption Policy (like using futures markets to reward and punish group leaders) • Rallying society around long-term "sacred" goals Source:

text: Delphi Digital: Zcash Advances Quantum-Resistant Roadmap, Recoverable Wallet Integration Underway Delphi Digital released a comprehensive report on Zcash (ZEC), noting that as Bitcoin is gradually absorbed by the traditional financial system and lacks native privacy, Zcash is being repositioned as its privacy and quantum-resistant complement. The report highlights that Zcash's shielded pool supply has surged from ~11% in early 2025 to ~30%, indicating a growing demand for privacy usage. Concurrently, Zcash is advancing its quantum-resistant roadmap, expecting to achieve protocol-level post-quantum privacy via the Tachyon upgrade by late 2026, advancing quantum-recoverable wallet integration in 2026, and perfecting post-quantum soundness in the long term. — link

text: Aave: rsETH Recovery Enters Phase II, Attacker's Positions Liquidated Aave has released Phase II of its rsETH recovery plan, confirming that the attacker's Aave V3 positions were liquidated on May 6. While the Arbitrum DAO voted to return $71 million in recovered ETH to Aave users, the funds were temporarily frozen by plaintiffs holding a judgment against North Korea. A court has now permitted the transfer of these restricted assets to Aave LLC. In the meantime, Aave will borrow funds to cover the shortfall until the assets are legally returned. Furthermore, Aave will burn the liquidated rsETH to offset the hacker's inflated supply and gradually restore bridge withdrawals and normal operations. — link

text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Bitcoin Frees Your Money From Any Single Bank In a May 1 interview with Peter McCormack, Strategy founder Michael Saylor said the core idea of Bitcoin is to let people who do not trust banks, governments, or even each other build a "viral bank" in cyberspace, maintained collectively by everyone. He argued that Bitcoin is the best technology today for transferring economic energy, because capital cannot rely on any single country or bank over the long term and needs the ability to move quickly. On the Bitcoin network, users can rapidly transfer assets when a crisis comes, instead of being locked into one jurisdiction or institutional system. — link
